role: A React SVG visualization component that renders the two-gravity separator form, mapping projected points into left/right basins, animating them, and exposing the balance bar, pole labels, and separation verdict.
capabilities: Render two-basin separator SVG canvas sized via useSize
Project points by pole/lean into x/y coordinates around a center line
Animate points with framer-motion AnimatePresence and motion feel tokens
Provide large transparent hit targets for point selection
Display diverging balance bar and numeric lean counts
Show pole A/B labels and a reset-to-default-poles button
Render the engine separation verdict (separates / does not separate)
Stamp semantic ui://instrument/separator and per-point addresses
substance_why: The file implements a complete visual component: geometry, animation, hit layer, labels, reset control, and verdict rendering, all driven by projection data.
interactions_present: Click/tap invisible SVG hit circles to select a point via onPick
Click reset button to restore default poles via onClearPoles
interactions_missing: Hover or tooltip detail on points
Accessible live announcement of the separation verdict
Loading/empty state when projection or separation data is absent
performance_risks: Animating every projected point with AnimatePresence/motion without virtualization
Iterating the full point list twice (decorative layer + hit layer)
Using layoutId per point which can stress layout animations on large sets
SVG viewBox tied to measured pixel size causing full re-render on every resize
No memoization of place() geometry or derived balance-bar dimensions
reuse_verdict_why: The two-gravity concept and data mapping are valuable to keep, but the component should be refactored for accessibility, performance, and clearer separation of concerns.
decomposition_hints: Split SVG rendering from HTML overlay controls (labels, reset, verdict, counts)
Extract geometry/layout math into a dedicated hook or utility
Separate decorative animated point layer from pointer-hit layer
Make the balance bar its own component
Add a small presentational point/item component to avoid inline map closures
